title: Space acquisition and NRO picks face SASC
summary: Erich Hernandez-Baquero and Roger Mason, nominated for key space and NRO leadership roles, testified before the Senate Armed Services Committee. Their nominations aim to advance U.S. space acquisition and intelligence satellite programs.
company: U.S. Air Force and National Reconnaissance Office

title: Antaris™ Establishes Aeonyx™ to Advance All-Domain Mission Virtualization for Defense
summary: Antaris™ announced the creation of Aeonyx™, a new company focused on advancing mission virtualization for defense applications. This strategic reorganization aims to help defense organizations evaluate architectures and improve operational performance.

title: U.K. government preparing to release new space strategy
summary: The U.K. government is set to unveil a new space strategy that emphasizes a coordinated, government-wide approach to space activities. This initiative aims to streamline and enhance the country's space sector policies and operations.

title: Flexell Space raises $20 Million in Series A round
summary: Flexell Space, a developer of next-generation space solar solutions, raised $20 million in a Series A funding round. The company is based in Uiwang, South Korea.

title: Defense spending lifts Europe’s space economy
summary: European government space spending increased by 12% to $15.4 billion in 2025, driven by rising defense budgets, despite a 3% global decline. This trend was highlighted in a July 13 report by the European Space Agency.

title: L3Harris, Sierra Space to build 36 satellites to expand U.S. missile-tracking network
summary: L3Harris and Sierra Space have been awarded a combined $1.75 billion contract by the Space Development Agency to build 36 satellites for expanding the U.S. missile-tracking network. This initiative aims to enhance the country's space-based missile defense capabilities.

title: Poland to host new ESA center as government boosts investments in space
summary: Poland will host the first European Space Agency (ESA) center in an eastern flank member state, focusing on civil security and resilience. The move aligns with Poland's increased investments in the space sector.

title: Accel and Prosus backed QOSMIC raises $3.33 Million to Build Optical Ground Stations for the Orbital Data Economy
summary: QOSMIC secured $3.33 million in seed funding led by Accel and Prosus to develop optical ground stations for the orbital data economy. The investment includes participation from South Park Commons, ARTPARK, and an angel investor.

title: Reditus Space completes first reentry vehicle
summary: Reditus Space, a startup focused on recoverable and reusable spacecraft, has completed its first reentry vehicle. The vehicle is scheduled for launch later this year.

title: Voyager completes acquisition of Astrobotic
summary: Voyager Technologies has finalized the acquisition of Astrobotic Technology, a lunar infrastructure company. Astrobotic recently secured two NASA lunar lander missions.

title: SpaceX launches flight-proven rocket for 600th time, sending Starlink satellites to orbit
summary: SpaceX launched Starlink satellites using two Falcon 9 rockets eight hours apart, with the second launch marking the 600th reuse of a flight-proven booster.

title: Scientists spot 4 superdense stellar corpses hiding behind their red dwarf companions
summary: Scientists discovered four superdense stellar corpses (white dwarfs) hidden behind their red dwarf companions, which were previously undetectable due to their obscured visibility.

title: The 1st of 10,000 'missing' black holes in the Omega Centauri star cluster has been found by the Hubble and James Webb space telescopes
summary: The Hubble and James Webb space telescopes have discovered the first of 10,000 missing black holes in the Omega Centauri star cluster. This breakthrough highlights the collaborative efforts of the two telescopes in advancing astronomical research.

title: 'Once Upon a Time in Space': How to watch PBS's out-of-this-world astronaut documentary
summary: PBS is airing a new astronaut documentary titled 'Once Upon a Time in Space,' offering a visually stunning portrayal of space exploration. The film is described as captivating and beautiful, akin to a journey to Oz.

title: 1st-ever X-rays in space offer hope for possible patients headed to the moon
summary: A portable X-ray device has been successfully used in orbit for the first time, offering potential medical and technical applications for future lunar missions. This breakthrough could enable X-ray diagnosis of injuries, equipment damage, or satellite issues in space.

title: This cosmic 'lighthouse' is blazing a magnetic trail through the Milky Way
summary: Astronomers have directly mapped the magnetic field around an unusual pulsar in the Milky Way, confirming a decades-old prediction about particle streaming from the spinning stellar remnant.

title: Honeycomb structures spotted on Mars | Space photo of the day for July 14, 2026
summary: A space photo from July 14, 2026, shows honeycomb-like structures on Mars, sparking curiosity about their origin. The image was featured as the Space Photo of the Day.

title: The biggest skywatching day of 2026 is coming. Here's what you'll see in North America and Europe
summary: A major skywatching event is expected in 2026, with visible celestial phenomena on August 12 across North America and Europe. Details include what observers in Spain, Iceland, the U.K., and North America will see and when.

title: How to watch SpaceX launch Starship Flight 13 on July 16
summary: SpaceX is set to launch its 13th Starship test flight on July 16, featuring the first deployment of Starlink V3 satellites and testing of booster recovery and upgraded flight systems. This mission marks a significant step toward operational Starship missions.

title: Moon landings could destroy evidence of life's origins
summary: Moon landings may inadvertently destroy evidence of life's origins, prompting concerns about preserving scientific data and investments in space exploration.

title: NASA Invites Media to Serbia Artemis Accords Signing Ceremony
summary: Serbia is set to sign the Artemis Accords during a ceremony at NASA Headquarters in Washington, hosted by NASA Deputy Administrator Matt Anderson and attended by Serbian and U.S. officials.

title: NASA Astronaut Anil Menon, Crewmates Arrive at Space Station
summary: NASA astronaut Anil Menon and two Roscosmos cosmonauts arrived at the International Space Station aboard the Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft. The mission increased the station's crew to 10 for the next two weeks.

title: Hubble Sees Crimson Cloud and Stars
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured a stunning image of stellar nursery LH 95 in the Large Magellanic Cloud, showcasing crimson gas and blue-white stars. The region features both low-mass infant stars and massive blue giants.

title: NASA Jets Turn Red, White, and Blue
summary: NASA repainted two iconic aircraft, an F-15 and an F/A-18, in red, white, and blue to celebrate America's 250th birthday ahead of a July 4, 2026 flyover in Washington. The aircraft, from NASA's Armstrong Flight Research Center, will participate in the patriotic event.

title: NASA’s Roman Telescope Will Spot Distant Black Holes That Shred Stars
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope will help scientists detect and study distant black holes that shred stars to understand their formation and growth over time. The telescope aims to observe supermassive black holes from the early universe.

title: Fans of the Arctic
summary: The Arctic sediment from ice-capped mountains spreads across a broad river valley on Russia’s Severny Island. This geological phenomenon highlights the dynamic processes of erosion and sediment deposition in Arctic environments.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4941-4947: (Pin)Stripes on the Fourth of July
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover is approaching a geologic boundary on Mars ahead of the Fourth of July holiday, observing polygonal patterns and transitioning between sandy and bedrock regions. The rover's activities are documented in a blog post by a NASA JPL team member.

title: NASA Study Points to Smoother Air Taxi Rides
summary: NASA research aims to improve passenger comfort in air taxis to boost willingness to fly. The study provides data to help plan smoother rides for the emerging eVTOL industry.

title: NASA Astronaut Anil Menon
summary: NASA astronaut Anil Menon is set to launch aboard the Roscosmos Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft to the International Space Station on July 14, 2026, alongside cosmonauts Pyotr Dubrov and Anna Kikina. This mission will join the ongoing Expedition aboard the ISS.

title: NASA’s Hubble Discovers First of Star Cluster’s Missing Black Holes
summary: Astronomers using Hubble and Webb data have discovered the first stellar-mass black hole in the Omega Centauri star cluster. This marks a significant advancement in understanding black holes within star clusters.

title: Soyuz MS-29 launches new crew to ISS from Baikonur
summary: Soyuz MS-29 successfully launched a new crew to the International Space Station (ISS) from the Baikonur Cosmodrome on July 14. This marks another crewed mission to the orbital laboratory.

title: Launch Preview: Soyuz to launch crew to ISS, Starship to fly another test flight
summary: Soyuz is set to launch a crew to the International Space Station (ISS), while SpaceX's Starship is preparing for its 13th flight test. Seven launches are scheduled worldwide this week.

title: NASA’s Roman telescope into prelaunch servicing following arrival in Florida
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center for prelaunch servicing. This marks a key milestone ahead of its upcoming launch.

title: Booster 20 completes record duration Static Fire
summary: Booster 20, a SpaceX rocket, completed a record-duration static fire test with all 33 engines. This marks a significant milestone in SpaceX's development efforts.

title: Rocket Lab sees Electron gains, Neutron delays in first half of 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket achieved record performance in the first half of 2026, while the development of its Neutron rocket faced delays.

title: Progress at the Cape: New Launch Pads rise for Stoke, Relativity
summary: The Space Coast is seeing significant progress with new launch pads being built for Stoke Space and Relativity Space, marking a continued revitalization of the region. Multiple launch providers are advancing their facilities at Cape Canaveral.

title: Launch Preview: Long March 10B and Vikram-I debut
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled internationally this week, including the debut of China's Long March 10B and India's Vikram-I rockets.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward its planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved key milestones. The station aims to be the world's first commercial space station.

title: Starship in Florida pushing for launch this year
summary: SpaceX is accelerating preparations for Starship launches from Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch this year. The company and its contractors are making progress toward this goal.

title: NASA announces new lander contracts for Moon Base Phase One
summary: NASA announced four new lunar lander contracts as part of its Moon Base Phase One initiative in June 2026. The contracts aim to support the development of landers for future lunar missions.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, but no specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting events to discuss the implementation of decisions from the CM25 ministerial meeting. These events will outline key priorities and opportunities for industry engagement in ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new advanced robotic arm designed for space applications. This innovation aims to enhance robotic operations in space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft using young researchers to ensure mission success in space. The team focuses on innovative solutions to withstand the harsh space environment.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The article discusses a sturdy and sensitive component in the space industry, though specific details are not provided.

title: Payload Field Guide: NSSL
summary: The U.S. Space Force expanded its National Security Space Launch (NSSL) program by adding two new launch providers on July 8, increasing the total number of certified companies to seven.

title: Spinifex Space Launches With Australian Test Ranges
summary: Spinifex Space has launched operations to offer end-to-end suborbital launch campaigns and private range access in Australia. The company will also provide test and evaluation infrastructure for space missions.

title: Lunar Outpost Joins ESA’s Moonraker Mission in 2030
summary: Lunar Outpost Europe will contribute thermal management solutions for ESA's Moonraker mission, which is scheduled for 2030 to operate in lunar orbit despite extreme temperature variations.

title: Reditus Finishes ENOS Reentry Vehicle Ahead of First Flight
summary: Reditus has completed the development of its first reentry spacecraft, ENOS, ahead of its scheduled debut flight this fall. The company announced the milestone today.

title: Reflect Orbital Cleared to Fly In-Orbit Mirror by FCC
summary: Reflect Orbital has received FCC clearance to launch an in-orbit mirror, despite concerns about its environmental and astronomical impact. The FCC stated that such concerns fall outside its regulatory scope.

title: Pulse Space Lands $40M Space Force Contract to Develop Laser Tech
summary: Pulse Space, a Washington-based startup, secured a $40 million contract from the U.S. Space Force to develop laser technology for beaming power to satellites in orbit. The funding will advance their mission to enhance satellite capabilities through wireless power transmission.

title: US Government Studying Offshore Launch Opportunities
summary: The U.S. government is exploring offshore launch opportunities by studying infrastructure development or repurposing on the Outer Continental Shelf for space launches and reentries. The Bureau of Ocean Energy Management (BOEM) issued a Request for Information (RFI) to gather relevant data.

title: NATO Allies Plan to Stitch Together Sovereign Space Fleets
summary: Eight NATO allies announced plans to integrate their national defense satellites into a unified network. This initiative aims to enhance collective space-based defense capabilities among member states.

title: Blue Origin Seeks $10B at $130B Valuation, Reporting Shows
summary: Blue Origin is seeking $10 billion in funding at a $130 billion valuation, marking a shift away from Jeff Bezos' sole financial support. The company is reportedly moving towards a more independent financial model.

title: Auxilium Demos Tech To Enable Sustainable Bioprinting in Orbit
summary: Auxilium Biotechnologies successfully created multiple types of organ tissues aboard the ISS, demonstrating scalable bioprinting technology in orbit. This achievement highlights potential future applications for sustainable bioprinting in space.

title: Honeywell to Lead ESA Quantum Space Magnetometer Contract
summary: Honeywell Aerospace will lead a consortium to develop a compact quantum space magnetometer for ESA, aiming to advance Earth's magnetic field research. The project involves partners Quantum Brilliance and Jagiellonian University.

title: NRL Awards Parsons $245M Satellite Ground Systems Contract for Tracking Facility
summary: The U.S. Naval Research Laboratory awarded Parsons Corporation a $245 million contract to support satellite ground systems software and operations at a tracking facility. The contract continues Parsons' work in this area.

title: Frontier to Adopt Starlink With New Customer Portal From SpaceX
summary: Frontier Airlines and other Indigo Partners airlines will adopt SpaceX's Starlink in-flight connectivity for over 1,000 aircraft. The deal includes a new customer portal for managing the service.

title: Iridium’s PNT Chip is Now Commercially Available
summary: Iridium has made its positioning, navigation, and timing (PNT) chip commercially available, with over 150 organizations showing interest. The chip was first announced in October.

title: L3Harris and Sierra Space Receive $1.75B for SDA Tranche 3, Tracking Layer
summary: L3Harris Technologies and Sierra Space secured $1.75 billion in contracts for the Space Development Agency's Tranche 3 Tracking Layer satellites. The funds will support rapid prototyping and expedited launches under fixed-price OTA contracts.

title: The Implications of Dual-Use for Protecting Space Assets
summary: Europe has historically separated civil space programs from defense, with clear distinctions between government and commercial roles. However, the growing dual-use nature of space assets is challenging these traditional divisions.

title: Ravee Optics Secures $6 Million Seed Round to Build Compact Laser Terminals
summary: Ravee Optics, a startup developing compact laser terminals, has secured $6 million in an oversubscribed seed funding round. The company aims to advance its technology for space applications.

title: Sonatel Taps Eutelsat OneWeb for Teleport Upgrade
summary: Sonatel, Senegal's leading telecommunications company, has completed a teleport upgrade in collaboration with Eutelsat OneWeb. The upgrade aims to enhance connectivity for the country's population of over 19 million people.

title: FCC Approves Reflect Orbital Demo Satellite to Test Redirecting Sunlight
summary: The FCC has approved Reflect Orbital's demonstration satellite to test redirecting sunlight back to Earth using a solar reflector. This marks a significant step in space-based solar energy experiments.

title: Space42 and Skylo Test D2D Test on Thuraya-4
summary: Space42 and Skylo successfully tested direct-to-device messaging on the Thuraya-4 satellite, demonstrating SMS and SOS capabilities. The partners announced the achievement on July 9 and plan to expand the service.
